message: Hi! This is your Daily Briefing. Feel free to ask me anything about today's market updates, mortgage rates, opportunities, risks, financing, rental markets, commercial real estate, insurance, regulations, or any other topic covered in today's briefing.

sections.title: INSURANCE & TAXES
sections.content: Insurance Trends: Insurance affordability remains most strained in hurricane-, wind-, hail- and wildfire-exposed areas. Florida, Louisiana and Oklahoma have the highest premium burdens relative to income, while parts of southern coastal states recorded inflation-adjusted increases of at least 25% from 2019 through 2024. Property Tax Trends: Assessments may continue reflecting prior price appreciation even as sales activity weakens. Commercial owners should also watch for local tax-burden shifts where declining office assessments reduce municipal revenue.Recommended Actions: Requote coverage 60–90 days before renewal, verify replacement-cost estimates, examine percentage-based catastrophe deductibles, confirm roof settlement terms, document mitigation improvements and appeal unsupported assessments.

sections.title: ACTION ITEMS
sections.content:
1. Request three same-day mortgage or home-equity quotes...
2. Audit all insurance policies...
3. Review every loan maturing within 24 months...
4. Compare your property...
5. Identify completed builder inventory...

sections.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
sections.content: U.S. real estate enters the week with mortgage rates at 6.55%, their highest level in nearly a year, and pending home sales down 5.4% in June. Buyers have greater negotiating leverage, but affordability remains strained by financing, insurance and taxes. Housing starts rose 19% to 1.427 million, although the increase was driven by multifamily projects; single-family starts and permits declined, limiting future for-sale supply. Fixed home-equity loans average roughly 8.12%, making them preferable to cash-out refinancing only when preserving a low-rate first mortgage creates clear savings. Commercial conditions remain uneven: the CMBS delinquency rate improved to 7.35%, but office distress remains severe. Insurance risk is concentrated in Florida, Louisiana, Oklahoma and other catastrophe-exposed markets. Owners should compare lenders, review insurance terms, preserve inexpensive debt, stress-test rental supply and begin commercial refinancing well before maturity.
